title: S2: per-anchor fidelity floor + drift telemetry + unparsed_blocks made mandatory
action: Per F11, F12, and the f2-1b judge's residual gap. Three additions to scripts/okf_migrate.mjs's coverage gate, all proven against the two already-migrated areas WITHOUT editing their concepts. (1) F11 FIDELITY FLOOR: for every anchor, compute normalized token overlap between the anchor's text in the PINNED BLOB and the body of the concept that claims it in bee.sources. Normalization: lowercase, strip markdown emphasis/backticks/punctuation, split on whitespace, drop a small stopword set, dedupe. Overlap = |anchor_tokens intersect concept_tokens| / |anchor_tokens|. Below 0.60 fails the gate NAMING the anchor, its owner, and the measured ratio. CRITICAL TUNING RULE: advisor-protocol's 26 anchors and the 47 patterns must ALL pass at 0.60 with ZERO edits to their concepts — if any fails, the normalization is wrong, not the concept; fix the normalization, never the content, and never lower the threshold to make it pass. Report the min/median overlap per area so the margin is visible. (2) F12 DRIFT TELEMETRY: per area report anchors_per_concept and concepts_per_100_source_lines; compare against the running median of already-pinned areas and fail when an area is an outlier (>2x or <0.5x the median) — with fewer than 3 pinned areas there is no median yet, so report only, never fail. Also run the whole-bundle invariants every check: authority uniqueness, zero not_canonical, index freshness (shell out to `bee knowledge check --json` and `bee knowledge index --check`), failing the gate if the bundle is unhealthy. (3) MANDATORY unparsed_blocks: derivePin must REFUSE a pin whose expected_counts omits unparsed_blocks (PIN_INCOMPLETE), closing the judge's gap — a pin omitting it currently passes. RED-FIRST: write tests first and capture the red — a deliberately summarized concept (fixture) falls below the floor and is named; both shipped areas pass unedited at 0.60 with their min/median quoted; a pin missing unparsed_blocks is refused; an outlier area fails only once >=3 medians exist; an unhealthy bundle (doctored not_canonical fixture) fails the gate.

truths: red evidence: the new tests failed before implementation (verbatim)
advisor-protocol's 26 anchors and the 47 patterns ALL pass the 0.60 floor with ZERO concept edits — min and median overlap quoted per area in the trace
a fixture concept that summarizes its anchor away falls below the floor and the failure NAMES the anchor, the owner, and the ratio — output quoted
a pin omitting expected_counts.unparsed_blocks is refused with PIN_INCOMPLETE — output quoted
telemetry reports anchors_per_concept and concepts_per_100_source_lines per area, and does NOT fail while fewer than 3 pinned areas exist
prohibitions: NEVER edit a migrated concept to make the floor pass — the normalization is the variable, the content is not
NEVER lower the 0.60 threshold to make a shipped area pass
do not change the PASS/FAIL wording the chain entries depend on

trace.outcome: S2: per-anchor fidelity floor (F11, 0.60), drift telemetry + whole-bundle invariants (F12), and expected_counts.unparsed_blocks made mandatory — both shipped areas clear the floor unedited (advisor-protocol min 0.977/median 1.000; critical-patterns min 0.795/median 0.907)
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "PRIOR BEHAVIOR, captured verbatim before implementation (.bee/tmp/f2-2-red.txt, exit 1, 22 assertion failures then a hard TypeError). The gate as shipped by f2-1b proved an anchor was CLAIMED but never that it was CARRIED, and it let a pin opt out of the format-blindness guard silently. Three specific prior behaviors:\n(1) `node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --derive '<pin with expected_counts {behaviors:4,rules:9,edges:6,pointers:7,total:26} and NO unparsed_blocks>'` exited 0 and printed `\"ok\": true` — the judge's residual gap: a pin omitting unparsed_blocks passed. Red run recorded: `FAIL test_okf_pins: CLI --derive exits 1 on a pin missing unparsed_blocks / exit 0`.\n(2) The extraction carried anchor IDS only, never anchor TEXT — `TypeError: M.normalizeTokens is not a function` and `FAIL test_okf_pins: the ba-nine-section extraction returns an anchor->text Map`. With no bytes there was no per-anchor fidelity measurement possible at all, so a concept that summarised its anchor into one sentence still satisfied set-equality and passed the gate green.\n(3) No drift telemetry and no whole-bundle invariants ran on --check: `FAIL ... must export collectTelemetry / telemetryIssues / bundleInvariantIssues / runBundleInvariants`. A bundle with a duplicated bee.authoritative_for or a not_canonical concept stayed green in the coverage gate forever, because `knowledge check` reports both as PROFILE WARNINGS (D13, non-strict in the chain).\nAll nine new exports were absent from scripts/okf_migrate.mjs at HEAD 53b4bd2.",
  "green_evidence": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> `PASS run_verify: 66 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=58418ms`, exit 0 (.bee/tmp/f2-2-verify.txt). scripts/test_okf_pins.mjs: `PASS test_okf_pins: 167 assertions`, up from 145.",
  "tuning_rule_evidence": "F11's floor is 0.60 and BOTH shipped areas clear it with ZERO edits to any migrated concept and no threshold change. Measured margins: advisor-protocol n=26 min=0.977 median=1.000 max=1.000; critical-patterns n=47 min=0.795 median=0.907 max=0.959. The medians sit far clear of the floor, which is the signal that the normalization is not too strict. git status shows only scripts/okf_migrate.mjs and scripts/test_okf_pins.mjs modified — no file under docs/knowledge/ was touched.",
  "discrimination_evidence": "The floor discriminates rather than merely detecting absence. Fixture 1 (anchor summarised away entirely): `FIDELITY BELOW FLOOR: R4 (owner docs/knowledge/areas/x/summarised.md) retains 0.000 normalized token overlap ... 27 of 27 anchor tokens are absent`. Fixture 2 (a plausible paraphrase that keeps the gist and drops the specifics): `FIDELITY BELOW FLOOR: R4 (owner docs/knowledge/areas/x/paraphrased.md) retains 0.296 ... 19 of 27 anchor tokens are absent: r4, staleness, event, based, four, events, never, time, limit, verb, refuses, missing, stale, corrective, message`. A faithful re-wording of the same anchor passes.",
  "telemetry_evidence": "node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --telemetry -> pinned_areas 2, median_available false; advisor-protocol anchors_per_concept 6.50 / concepts_per_100_source_lines 1.97 (26 anchors, 4 concepts, 203 lines); critical-patterns 0.96 / 6.96 (47 anchors, 49 concepts, 704 lines). With 2 < 3 pinned areas telemetry REPORTS ONLY and never fails, asserted directly (`with fewer than 3 pinned areas telemetry reports only`), and the >2x and <0.5x sides both fail once 3 samples exist.",
  "bundle_invariant_evidence": "End-to-end, not only the pure function: test section 22 drops one deliberately non-canonical concept into docs/knowledge/areas/advisor-protocol/ and asserts `--check advisor-protocol` flips exit 0 -> exit 1 naming BUNDLE UNHEALTHY, then removes it and asserts green again.",
  "chain_wording_evidence": "The PASS/FAIL wording the chain entries depend on is byte-unchanged: `PASS okf_migrate --check advisor-protocol: ` and `PASS okf_migrate --check-patterns: ` still match their anchored regexes; the new fidelity/telemetry/bundle lines are additional indented lines below them."
}
